Farn Polrrrcel PRACTTcEs C ouvrrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramenro, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
March 14,2007
Brian Ramsey
392Main Street
Weed, California 96094
RE: Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. 4-07-029
Dear Mr. Ramsey:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the post_
govemmental employment provisions of the Political Reform Act (the..i.ct,).1 please
note that nothing in this letter should be construed to evaluate any conduct *iri"h *uy
have already taken place. The Commission will not advise with iespect to past conduct.
(Regulation I 8 329(bX8XA).)
QUESTION
Upon leaving your position as a city councilmember, does the Act prevent you
from accepting aposition as a police dispatcher with the same city yor, ."."rtly served as
a councilmernber?
CONCLUSION
No. There is nothing the Act that prohibits you from accepting a police
dispatcher position now that you have left the city council.
FACTS
You served on the Weed City Council from March 2000 until December 2006.
The City of Weed advertised for a police dispatcher position in January 2007. you
I Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear attitle 2,sectiors
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.


FileNo. A-07-029
Page No. 2
applied and received_ the position of police dispatcher. You have numerous years of
experience in the field having previously worked for the Porterville police Department
and the Tulare County SherifPs Office. The City of Weed has requested that you provide
written confirmation from our office thatitis permissible for yo,, io accept this position,
and that it would not be prohibited under the Act. In our telephone convirsation, you
stated that the only reason you would appearbefore the city council once you became a
police dispatcher would be to conduct your police dispatching job duties.
ANALYSIS
There is nothing in the Act that prevents you from accepting employment as a
police dispatcher with the City of Weed once you have teft the city-council. However,
section 87406.3(a) places a restriction on local officials from appearing before their
former agency for aperiod of one year after leaving office o. "-ptoy-"rt under certain
conditions:
"A local elected official.. .. who held a position with a local
government agency as defined in Section 92041shall not, for a
period of one year after leaving that office or employment, act as
agent or attomey for, or otherwise represent, for compensation, any
other person, by making any formal or informal appearance beforg
or by making any oral or written communication to, that local
government agency, or any committee, subcommittee, orpresent
member of that local government agency, or any officer or
employee of the local government agencr if the appearance or
communication is made for the purpose of influencing
administrative or legislative action, or influencing any action or
proceeding involving the issuance, amendment, awarding, or
revocation of a permit, license, grant, or contract, or the sale or
purchase of goods or property.,,
However, under section 87406.3(b) this reshiction does not apply to any
individual who is, at the time of the appearance or communication be.forl his or her
former agency,".-. aboard member, officer, or employee of another local govemment
agency or an employee or representative of a public agency and is appearin! or
communicating on behalf of that agency.,,
As you stated, if you were to appear before the Weed City Council it would only
be in connection with your formal duties as a police dispatcher. 
-Therefore, 
you are not
reskicted from appearing before the city council in youi capacity as a police dispatcher
pursuant to the exception described in section 87406.3(b), as you *orld be an employee
of another local government agency appearing on behaif of that agency.


FileNo. A-07-029
Page No. 3
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
/Afd,u>(m,or-
By: Sukhi K. Brar
Counsel, Legal Division

CONCLUSION: No. There is nothing the Act that prohibits you from accepting a police
